title: androsace bisulca var. bisulca
intro: Androsace bisulca var. bisulca, a rare and lesser-known species within the Androsace genus, is a type of alpine plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is a small perennial herb with pink or white flowers that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. In terms of herbal preparations, Androsace bisulca var. bisulca is typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for medicinal use.
